text.title: figma-vs-adobe-strategy-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for figma-vs-adobe-strategy-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Recommending a single tool for teams with diverse needs (product designers, marketers, illustrators) and hoping "integrations" will bridge the gaps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forcing one tool on a team with both UI/UX and asset creation needs creates friction on both sides — product designers wait on render performance in Adobe, creatives struggle with Figma's simpler vector tools. Hybrid with a clear responsibility split is almost always more productive.

failure-mode.description: Agent comparison includes Adobe XD as a current product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adobe XD was deprecated in 2023; correct to Adobe Express or Creative Cloud apps. This is a persistent agent error that must be explicitly checked.

failure-mode.description: Agent makes feature parity claims without citing a verifiable source and date.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feature parity claims (e.g., "Figma now matches Adobe in vector tools") are frequently inaccurate by the time an agent is queried; require human fact-check against current product pages.

failure-mode.description: Agent underestimates migration effort for large Figma component libraries with complex variable/token structures.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents have no reliable model of Figma library migration complexity; always require a human estimate based on actual component and variable counts.

failure-mode.description: Agents underestimate the time and cost of hybrid workflow integration (Figma + Adobe + asset pipeline coordination).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hybrid setups sound pragmatic but add integration friction (file naming, export standards, team communication overhead, tool-switching context loss) that agents systematically underestimate.
